evidence: #242 v3d candidate ba54299f199df487bb9639d57f383cb7f15ac6d9 uses document-parse-260630 plus auto mode for contract-mode OCR.
The protected performance invariant is per-attempt OCR hard timeout: one OCR API call or one OCR attempt must not exceed 30 seconds.
The policy does not require the whole logical OCR workflow to finish within 30 seconds, and it does not treat total elapsed time above 100 seconds as an automatic failure.
Retry is intentionally allowed up to five attempts, so total logical OCR workflow time can exceed 100 seconds while each individual OCR attempt remains bounded to 30 seconds.
The v3d decision explicitly rejects document-parse-nightly, enhanced mode, 35-second parent timeout, and two-attempt or three-attempt retry policies for this issue.
Fail-first evidence used baseline b0c7f357912d33d816dddc19947d00a86494d723 and the same harness failed 3 of 7 tests before the fix.
Post-fix evidence used the same harness and passed 7 of 7 tests after applying document-parse-260630, auto mode, 30-second per-attempt timeout, and five retries.
Focused verification passed 143 Python tests for AutoClicker OCR policy and related static checks, and the backend OCR proxy Jest suite passed 31 tests.
liveOnlyStatus OPEN_UNTIL_LIVE_PASS remains required because installed AutoClicker live verification must still confirm the same per-attempt timeout policy in MetDO logs before #242 can close.
